name: Community Trust
description_short: Self-Help Federal Credit Union is a community-focused credit union with branches across six states offering member services including savings accounts, loans, and free financial coaching.
description_long: Self-Help Federal Credit Union operates as a federally-chartered credit union with a stated mission of creating and protecting ownership and economic opportunity for all, especially people of color, women, rural residents, and low-wealth families and communities. The organization maintains physical branch locations in California, Connecticut, Illinois, South Carolina, Washington, and Wisconsin, while also offering remote services including online and mobile banking to serve members nationwide. The credit union provides a full range of banking products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), money market accounts, and various loan products ranging from personal loans to commercial and small business lending. Self-Help distinguishes itself through a community-oriented approach, offering free financial coaching services, partnerships focused on economic opportunity expansion, and specialized loan programs such as discounted rates on electric or plug-in hybrid electric vehicle financing. The organization emphasizes transparent member support with published routing numbers (322273696), accessible fee schedules, and account disclosure documents available in both English and Spanish. However, the website evidence is limited to their main homepage and shows some broken internal links, making comprehensive service details difficult to fully verify from the provided content.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificate of Deposit (CD) products with stated rates
Free financial coaching and credit building resources
Small business loans and commercial lending
Electric and plug-in hybrid vehicle financing with rate discounts
Account security resources and fraud protection guidance
Scholarship programs for members and community
pros: Offers free one-on-one financial coaching with expert coaches to help members with short and long-term financial goals
Provides competitive CD rates (3.82% APY for 1-year, 3.35% APY for 3-year certificates as of April 2026)
Offers discounted loan rates specifically for electric and plug-in hybrid electric vehicle purchases
Maintains physical branches in six states (CA, CT, IL, SC, WA, WI) plus remote online and mobile banking options
Provides small business lending and commercial loan products for entrepreneurs and business owners
Operates under a member-focused mission prioritizing underserved communities including people of color and low-wealth families
Offers scholarship programs through Self-Help Scholarship with annual application periods
cons: Website navigation shows broken links to member support resources (404 errors on FAQ and support center pages)
Limited service detail visibility on website makes it difficult to compare all product offerings and fees
Geographic service limitations with physical branches in only 6 states, though remote services partially offset this
best_for: Community-minded consumers prioritizing ethical banking with social mission alignment
Small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking personalized loan underwriting and commercial financing
Low-to-moderate income families seeking free financial coaching and credit-building support
diagnosis: Self-Help Federal Credit Union is best for consumers seeking community-oriented banking with a social mission focus, particularly those from underserved communities, small business owners, and individuals wanting free financial coaching. The main caveat is that geographic reach is limited despite online options, and website navigation issues make it difficult to fully verify current product details and pricing beyond what's displayed on the homepage.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
